registry_meta.title: Seed control-plane artifact registry
registry_meta.status_note: artifact_status is independent from master-plan task status markers

validation_hook_standards.evidence_required: Required fields present for every artifact or artifact type record
Referenced artifact IDs and edge fields resolve
Source-authority references and material grounding are explicit
Terminology, dependencies, and boundaries are consistent with accepted upstream artifacts
Explicit human acceptance for downstream use
Revalidation required after validator or upstream acceptance change
Only used when real fixtures, harnesses, or test contracts exist

stale_rule_catalog.rule: Accepted downstream artifacts connected by hard dependency edges become stale on accepted source-authority change; reference-only links do not propagate by default.
Hard dependencies auto-stale on accepted upstream change; validator changes require revalidation.
Accepted upstream changes trigger stale review by default; deterministic derivations may opt into auto propagation per dependency edge.
Sync and delta artifacts summarize accepted truth and normally require human stale review on upstream change.
Reference-only edges do not propagate stale status by default.

artifact_types.name: Source authority document
artifact_types.purpose: External or mirrored authority document governing platform meaning

artifact_types.name: Master execution plan record
artifact_types.purpose: Working control document for the program

artifact_types.name: Control-plane spec
artifact_types.purpose: Normative spec for registry, rules, or operating artifacts

artifact_types.name: Control-plane dataset
artifact_types.purpose: Machine-readable registry, graph, or control-plane data

artifact_types.name: Canon or interpretation pack
artifact_types.purpose: Contradiction, glossary, or canon interpretation artifact

artifact_types.name: Semantic contract pack
artifact_types.purpose: Shared semantic contracts and state-machine packs

artifact_types.name: Technical architecture pack
artifact_types.purpose: Technical baseline, Platform Gate, topology, or interface pack

artifact_types.name: Release contract pack
artifact_types.purpose: Platform Gate or release-specific contract pack

artifact_types.name: Reusable execution or governance pack
artifact_types.purpose: Governance, proof, protocol, workflow, applet, or handoff pack

artifact_types.name: Surface or repo architecture pack
artifact_types.purpose: Surface contract, repo/package architecture, or documentation-plane artifact

artifact_types.name: Execution packet
artifact_types.purpose: Bounded implementation packet with whitelist and approval rules

artifact_types.name: Delta, sync, or stale-detection pack
artifact_types.purpose: Delta capture, recurring sync, stale rules, or gate re-check artifact

The accepted P1.2 note fixes projection-vs-ontology and release-order interpretation for run-class exposure.
The accepted P1.4 topology canon defines where run classes surface across releases and Task Studio handoff.

The accepted P1.6 scenario corpus defines the release-stage pathways the run classes and protocol defaults must support.
The primitive-set authority defines Run Class, Protocol, Workflow, and Applet as shared-environment primitives.
The release doctrine fixes Prompt Studio, governed applet chat, and Commissioning Bridge as required release stages.
The north-star authority fixes the V1 task-family set and commissioned-work orientation that this taxonomy formalizes.
The taxonomy must preserve no agent-builder-first, no transcript-first truth, no hidden memory sludge, and no app-private ontology drift.

notes: P2.2 accepted run-class taxonomy and protocol-kernel baseline mapping V1 Task Studio task families onto shared run classes.

artifacts.title: Phase 2 governance, authority, and writeback invariants

The accepted P2.2 taxonomy defines conservative authority and writeback defaults that these invariants make binding.
The accepted P1.2 note fixes projection-vs-ontology interpretation and the need for explicit commissioning semantics.
The accepted P1.4 topology canon defines where approval and governance surfaces appear across releases and handoffs.
The accepted P1.5 seam map fixes lane separation, authority/proof boundaries, and commissioned-work bridge seams as high-blast-radius semantics.
The accepted P1.6 scenarios and edge cases define the governance paths and failures these invariants must prevent.
The primitive-set authority defines Authority Scope, Review, Approval Decision, and Writeback Proposal as shared-environment primitives.
The release doctrine requires governed applet work and explicit commissioning semantics before Task Studio handoff.
The north-star authority requires explicit contract, authority, proof, delta, and lane-separated writeback for serious work.
The invariant pack must preserve no silent proposal application, no hidden memory sludge, no agent-builder-first, and no app-private ontology drift.

notes: P2.3 accepted invariant layer for scope widening, lane separation, approval points, policy boundaries, and authority semantics.
Accepted for downstream use as the Phase 2 governance baseline feeding release packs, reusable execution, and later full governance specs.

The accepted P1.6 scenarios and edge cases define replay, compare, contradiction, and proof-handling paths this pack must cover.
The primitive-set authority defines proof, validation, and variant/replay substrate concerns preserved here.
The release doctrine requires compare, artifact, governed applet, and commissioning stages to preserve task-shaped proof.
The north-star authority requires proof-returning serious work rather than generic explanation blobs.
The invariant pack must preserve no hidden memory sludge, no transcript-first rewrite trap, and no app-private proof shortcuts.

notes: P2.4 accepted invariant layer for proof expectations, uncertainty, omission, contradiction, replay/comparison expectations, and failure classes.
Accepted for downstream use as the Phase 2 proof baseline feeding release packs, Task Studio proof surfaces, and later full evaluation specs.

artifacts.title: Phase 2 chat-native semantic packs by release

dependency_refs.notes: P2.5 scope, sequencing, and carry-forward rules come from the master plan.
The semantic pack reuses accepted P0.1 artifact, validation-hook, and stale-rule semantics.
The accepted P1.1 glossary and contradiction register normalize release-local vocabulary and projection-vs-ontology tensions used here.
The accepted P1.2 note fixes the chat-native wedge, Task Studio north star, release order, and projection-vs-ontology rule.
The accepted P1.3 owner-layer map keeps release-local vocabulary additive to shared and app-level primitives instead of replacing them.
The accepted P1.4 topology canon defines the continuity centers, bridge releases, and handoff obligations that each semantic pack must preserve.
The accepted P1.5 seam map identifies R4 and R7 bridge semantics and shared-object continuity as high-blast-radius seams.
The accepted P1.6 scenarios and edge cases define the stage-by-stage semantic pathways and drift failures this pack must cover.
The accepted P2.1 object model defines the shared objects that each release pack must project without replacement.
The accepted P2.2 taxonomy defines the shared run classes that release packs may foreground but may not replace.
The accepted P2.3 invariants bind authority, approval, and lane-separation behavior across every release pack.
The accepted P2.4 invariants bind proof, uncertainty, omission, contradiction, replay, and failure semantics across every release pack.
The primitive-set authority defines the shared environment objects and projection grammar that the release packs must project.
The release doctrine defines the seven public chat-native releases and the view-specific vocabulary this pack formalizes semantically.
The north-star authority defines the commissioned-work handoff target that R7 must preserve without ontology translation.
The pack must preserve no master chat truth, no separate backend per view, no hidden memory sludge, and no agent-builder-first drift.

notes: P2.5 accepted semantic pack defining one release-specific semantic overlay for each public chat-native release from R1 through R7.
Accepted for downstream use as the Phase 2 release-semantics baseline feeding projection grammar, Phase 4 release packs, and the R7 -> Task Studio bridge.

artifacts.title: Phase 2 projection grammar contract pack

dependency_refs.notes: P2.6 scope, sequencing, and carry-forward rules come from the master plan.
The projection grammar pack reuses accepted P0.1 artifact, validation-hook, and stale-rule semantics.
The accepted P1.2 note fixes projection-vs-ontology rules and the chat-native -> Task Studio continuity that this grammar must preserve.
The accepted P1.4 topology canon defines the shared projection grammar set, stage emphasis, and cross-app handoff rules formalized here.
The accepted P1.5 seam map places projection changes above the high-blast-radius semantic seams and prevents surface-level truth drift.
The accepted P1.6 scenarios and edge cases define the surface transitions, alias mappings, and drift failures this pack must cover.
The accepted P2.1 object model defines the shared objects that these grammars must project without replacing.

The primitive-set authority defines Composer, Canvas, Inspector, Browser / Library, Timeline, Ledger, Compare View, Queue / Inbox, Board, Diff / Merge View, Live Monitor, and Console as shared projection grammar primitives.
The release doctrine defines what is shared across views, what is view-specific, and why projection grammar reuse matters during the chat-native phase.
The north-star authority defines Task Studio surface arrangements that must remain compositions of the shared grammar rather than a new semantic layer.
The pack must preserve no master chat truth, no separate backend per view, and no transcript-first rewrite trap through shared grammar reuse.
The accepted P1.3 owner-layer map informs non-owner boundaries so projection grammar does not transfer primitive ownership.
The accepted P2.5 release semantic packs inform stage-fit aliases and grammar composition without changing the P2.6 dependency order.

notes: P2.6 accepted projection grammar pack defining one reusable interaction grammar for shared surfaces across chat-native releases and Task Studio.
Accepted for downstream use as the Phase 2 surface-grammar baseline feeding Phase 3 architecture, Phase 4 release packs, and Phase 6 surface contracts.

artifacts.title: Phase 3 high-level technical architecture baseline

dependency_refs.notes: P3.1 scope, sequencing, and carry-forward rules come from the master plan.
The architecture pack reuses accepted P0.1 artifact, validation-hook, and stale-rule semantics.
The accepted P1.2 note fixes the chat-native wedge, Task Studio handoff, and projection-vs-ontology constraints that the runtime must preserve.
The accepted P1.3 owner-layer map constrains which runtime planes belong to engine, shared environment, and app/domain projections.
The accepted P1.4 topology canon defines release sequencing, continuity centers, and handoff obligations that the baseline runtime must support.
The accepted P1.5 seam map identifies the high-blast-radius semantics that must stay below client projection changes.
The accepted P1.6 scenarios and edge cases define the continuity, branching, artifact, reusable execution, and commissioning paths the runtime must support.
The accepted P2.1 object model defines the authoritative objects and lifecycle states that the runtime must persist and transport.
The accepted P2.2 taxonomy defines the typed execution classes and protocol defaults the worker and control planes must handle.
The accepted P2.3 invariants require explicit authority, review, and lane-separated writeback behavior in the runtime baseline.
The accepted P2.4 invariants require the runtime to preserve proof, validation, replay, and failure inspectability.
The accepted P2.5 release semantic packs define the stage-by-stage continuity and shared-object exposure that the runtime topology must support.
The accepted P2.6 projection grammar defines the client-facing grammar that the architecture must keep separate from shared object ownership.
The primitive-set authority defines engine layers, environment layers, storage classes, orchestration, provenance, and projection grammar capabilities.
The release doctrine defines the mandatory Platform Gate and the fixed public release order that the runtime must support.
The north-star authority defines the commissioned-work execution loop, proof surfaces, and writeback controls that later runtime phases must enable.
The baseline runtime must preserve no master chat truth, no separate backend per view, no transcript-first rewrite trap, and no repo-shape-driven architecture.

notes: P3.1 accepted technical baseline defining runtime planes, process families, hosted-default portability seams, and layer boundaries for the platform.
Accepted for downstream use as the Phase 3 architecture baseline feeding compiler, storage, API/event, Platform Gate, and later repo/package planning.
